input.title: Sermorelin as a growth hormone secretagogue: mechanisms, clinical evidence, and safety — a narrative review
input.abstract: Sermorelin (GHRH 1-29 NH₂) is a synthetic analogue of the first 29 amino acids of endogenous growth-hormone-releasing hormone. This narrative review summarizes its pharmacology, clinical evidence, and safety profile based on a PubMed search from 1980 to 2023 yielding 87 relevant publications. Sermorelin stimulates pituitary GH release via GHRH-R binding and has a shorter half-life (~10 minutes intravenous) than full-length GHRH. Clinical evidence includes use in adult GH deficiency, paediatric short stature, and anti-ageing contexts. Controlled trials demonstrate dose-dependent IGF-1 elevation; long-term safety data are limited to 12-month studies showing no pituitary adenoma risk. The review identifies key gaps: no head-to-head trials versus recombinant GH; absence of data in women >65 years; and a need for standardized dosing protocols.
